name: La Portiña Reservoir and Segurilla Watchtower Trail
name_original: Atalaya de Segurilla y vuelta al Embalse de la Portiña desde Segurilla
description: Hike 8.3 miles (13.3 km) to the historic Atalaya de Segurilla and scenic Embalse de la Portiña from Segurilla, rated moderate.
region_intro: Hiking around Segurilla offers diverse landscapes, from the serene waters of the Embalse de la Portiña to the historic Atalaya de Segurilla. The region is characterized by holm oak groves, rocky areas, and varied terrain within the broader Sierra de San Vicente. Trails often combine natural beauty with historical landmarks, providing a comprehensive outdoor experience. Best hiking trails around Segurilla The most popular hiking route is La Portiña Reservoir and Segurilla Watchtower Trail, an 8.3 miles (13.3 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 40 minutes to complete. This route combines scenic reservoir views with a visit to a 10th-century watchtower. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Portiña Reservoir loop from Embalse de La Portiña, an easy 4.6 miles (7.4 km) path. This trail offers pleasant walks around the reservoir, characterized by lush vegetation and holm oaks. Local hikers also love the Cruz de lo Llano – Riscal de Segurilla Mill loop from Segurilla, a 6.8 miles (10.9 km) trail leading through rocky areas and holm oak groves, often completed in about 3 hours 2 minutes. Hiking around Segurilla is defined by the Embalse de la Portiña, the historic Atalaya de Segurilla, and the extensive network of trails within the Sierra de San Vicente. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy reservoir loops to more challenging routes incorporating hills and historical sites. The routes in Segurilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Segurilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Segurilla?
A: Segurilla offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with nearly 80 routes available. These range from easy strolls around the reservoir to more challenging treks through the Sierra de San Vicente.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Segurilla?
A: Yes, Segurilla has numerous easy trails perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31461306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Portiña Reservoir loop from Embalse de La Portiña&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 7.4 km path offering pleasant walks around the reservoir, characterized by lush vegetation and holm oaks. There are 38 easy routes in total.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Segurilla?
A: Hiking in Segurilla offers diverse landscapes, from the serene waters of the Embalse de la Portiña to the historic Atalaya de Segurilla. You'll encounter holm oak groves, rocky areas known as 'berrocales', and varied terrain within the broader Sierra de San Vicente, including dehesas and pine forests.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks to explore on the trails around Segurilla?
A: Absolutely. A prominent historical landmark is the 10th-century Moorish watchtower, the Atalaya de Segurilla. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1308027156/la-portina-reservoir-and-segurilla-watchtower-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Portiña Reservoir and Segurilla Watchtower Trail&lt;/a&gt; combine scenic reservoir views with a visit to this historic site. You can also find other points of interest nearby, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5191721" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Francisco&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Segurilla?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Segurilla are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31461306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Portiña Reservoir loop from Embalse de La Portiña&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31461559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de lo Llano loop from Segurilla&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also part of the extensive 141 km Senda Viriato, a long-distance circular route through the Sierra de San Vicente.

Q: Is Segurilla suitable for dog-friendly hikes?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on trails can vary, the natural environment of Segurilla, with its open spaces, dehesas, and reservoir paths, generally offers good opportunities for hiking with dogs. Always ensure your dog is under control and check for any local signage regarding pet access, especially around wildlife habitats like the Embalse de la Portiña.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Segurilla?
A: The diverse landscapes of Segurilla make it suitable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Even in winter, the trails can be enjoyable, though it's always advisable to check local weather conditions and trail accessibility before heading out.

Q: Are there options for longer, more challenging hikes in Segurilla?
A: Yes, for those seeking more challenging routes, Segurilla offers several moderate to difficult trails. The region is integrated into the Sierra de San Vicente, which features a network of trails including sections of the Senda Viriato, a 141 km long-distance route. There are 36 moderate and 4 difficult routes available.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Segurilla?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 90 reviews. Hikers often praise the combination of natural beauty, such as the serene Embalse de la Portiña, and the historical significance of sites like the Atalaya de Segurilla. The varied terrain and well-marked paths are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or places for birdwatching in Segurilla?
A: Yes, the Atalaya de Segurilla, perched on a hill at 623 meters above sea level, offers commanding views of the surrounding area. The Embalse de la Portiña is also an excellent spot for birdwatching, with observation posts available and sightings of herons, coots, and mallards common.

Q: Are there any trails that combine water features like a reservoir or river with historical sites?
A: Many trails in Segurilla beautifully combine natural water features with historical landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1308027156/la-portina-reservoir-and-segurilla-watchtower-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Portiña Reservoir and Segurilla Watchtower Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering both scenic reservoir views and a visit to the ancient Atalaya de Segurilla.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking in Segurilla?
A: The Embalse de la Portiña is a haven for wildlife. Hikers often report sightings of various bird species like herons, coots, and mallards. Mammals such as foxes and wild boars are also known to inhabit the vicinity, particularly in the lush vegetation surrounding the reservoir.
tips: A tower built in the 10th century with the objective of surveillance and defense of the surrounding territories. A place at the top of the town of Segurilla from which you can see the entire Talavera region, the Portiña reservoir, etc.
La Portiña is a small water reservoir located north of Talavera de la Reina on the Portiña stream and next to the natural environment of the Berrocal mountain range.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: Based on Komoot data, this hike typically takes around 3 hours and 40 minutes to complete. The total distance is approximately 13.3 kilometers.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate. While initial sections are relatively flat, the ascent to the Segurilla Watchtower involves a stony and consistent incline. It is suitable for those with some hiking experience.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlights include the historic Segurilla Watchtower, an ancient Muslim defensive structure offering panoramic views, and the scenic La Portiña Reservoir, a protected wetland with rich biodiversity.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail begins with about 3 kilometers of relatively flat track leading to the reservoir. It then borders the La Portiña Reservoir for another 3 kilometers. The ascent to the Segurilla Watchtower is a 200-meter stretch on a stony path through a holm oak grove.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, please ensure your dog is kept on a leash, especially around the La Portiña Reservoir, which is a protected natural area with diverse wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or access the Segurilla Watchtower or La Portiña Reservoir areas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn are particularly pleasant due to milder temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can be hot, so an early start is recommended, while winter offers a different, often quieter, experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details are not provided, Segurilla is the starting point. Look for public parking options within the village or near the main access roads leading towards the reservoir or watchtower.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The La Portiña Reservoir is a protected wetland, home to various aquatic fauna, birds, and plant species. You might spot different bird species, especially around the water, and enjoy the pine and poplar forests.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restrooms or cafes along the trail?
faqs.answer: There are no specific facilities like restrooms or cafes directly on the trail itself. It is advisable to use facilities in Segurilla before starting your hike and to carry enough water and snacks for the duration of your trip.

faqs.question: Is this a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is designed as a circular hike, starting and ending in Segurilla, taking you to the Segurilla Watchtower and around the La Portiña Reservoir before returning to the village.
